title: XLOOKUP vs INDEX/MATCH: is there still a reason to nest?
meta_desc: XLOOKUP vs INDEX MATCH compared: capability parity, two-dimensional lookups, portability to old versions, and executed compatibility results for all three apps.
intro: XLOOKUP does in one call nearly everything INDEX/MATCH does in two — so the real question is portability: INDEX/MATCH works in every spreadsheet ever shipped; XLOOKUP needs modern versions (LibreOffice 24.8+, verified by execution).

cols: INDEX with 0 as row/col can, awkwardly

when.use_when: Modern-version environments — simpler to read, safer defaults, built-in not-found handling.
Maximum portability (old Excel, LibreOffice 24.2, unknown recipients), or two-dimensional lookups where INDEX with two MATCHes reads naturally.

compat_note: Executed results: INDEX and MATCH work in every LibreOffice release we test; XLOOKUP first works in LibreOffice 24.8 and returns #NAME? in 24.2. All are available in current Google Sheets and Excel 365; only INDEX/MATCH exists in Excel 2019 and earlier.
